output: Implemented batch 2 (T4-T5), verified the low-level shot hook and cache utils modules with Vitest, verified repository type safety with `npx tsc --noEmit`, and checkpointed progress to `.megaplan/plans/fix-the-fundamental-cache-20260406-0236/execution_batch_2.json`.
deviations: The batch 1 compatibility shim remains in `CreateShotWithGenerationsPathInput`, and `createShotWithGenerationsPath` now throws a clear error if the new RPC mutation is not supplied. The actual UI wiring for that input lands in T6, which is outside this batch.

To satisfy T5's `refetchType: 'inactive'` requirement without bypassing the shared helper, `invalidateShotsQueries` was extended with an optional filters parameter. Existing callers remain source-compatible.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added `useCreateShotWithGenerations` in the low-level `shots/` hook layer. It calls `supabase().rpc('create_shot_with_generations', ...).single()`, treats the returned `data` as the jsonb payload itself, guards on `success`, and invalidates inactive shot-list plus unified-project queries on success. Verified with the full existing `useShotCreation` hook test file and `npx tsc --noEmit`.

Rewrote `createShotWithGenerationsPath` to consume the atomic RPC result instead of creating a partial shot plus serial `insertAutoPositionedShotGeneration` calls. The path now maps RPC rows into `GenerationRow` objects with the same compatibility fields as `mapShotGenerationToRow`, computes shot image stats with the same unique-generation logic as `useShotsQueries`, upserts the fully hydrated shot into cache, enqueues generation invalidation with `includeShots: false`, and triggers background inactive shot refetch without the old `setTimeout` glue. Verified with TypeScript and manual field-by-field cross-check against the mapper/query logic.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: The new mutation uses the same `.rpc(...).single()` call pattern as `useCreateShotWithImage`, but because this RPC returns `jsonb`, the typed payload is the `data` object itself. The hook now parses `data as CreateShotWithGenerationsRpcResult | null`, checks `success`, and leaves the outer `{ data, error }` envelope at the Supabase client layer.

The rewritten path maps every RPC `shot_generation` into the same downstream-compatible fields that `mapShotGenerationToRow` produces: `id`, `generation_id`, deprecated aliases, `location`, `imageUrl`, `thumbUrl`, `type`, `created_at`, `createdAt`, `starred`, `name`, `based_on`, `params`, `timeline_frame`, `primary_variant_id`, and computed `position`. It also sets `metadata: {}` because the RPC payload does not include metadata, which keeps consumers that expect an object-shaped metadata field from receiving `undefined`.
